# Ki Browser — Complete Documentation

> A private web browser for iPhone. Built around the user, not the advertiser.

For the summary, see: [llms.txt](https://kibrowser.com/llms.txt)

## Product Overview

Ki is a privacy-first web browser for iPhone. The product principle is that a browser should stay out of your way: trackers do not load, no analytics SDK reports your activity, and there is no Ki account or backend. Bookmarks, history, tabs, and per-site settings live only on the device.

## Core Features

### Tracker blocking (default-on)
- Built on iOS's WKContentRuleList content-blocker mechanism.
- Blocks known cross-site trackers and ad networks before they load.
- No SDK touches the page; rules are bundled with the app.

### Per-site privacy controls
- Tap the shield in the address bar to grant or revoke any permission for that origin.
- Decisions are remembered until the user changes them.
- Per-origin toggles include cookies, scripts, autoplay, popups, microphone, and camera.

### Privacy profiles
- Multiple independent profiles — research, shopping, personal — each with its own cookie jar, history, and bookmarks.
- Switch between them in Settings.
- "Reset this profile" removes a profile's tracked state so a returning visit looks new to a site.

### Reader mode
- Strip a page down to text and images for sites that fight you.

### Search
- User chooses the default search engine in Settings.

### On-device by default
- Bookmarks, history, tabs, downloads, and per-site permissions are stored only on the user's iPhone.
- No iCloud sync in v1 (under consideration).
- Deleting the app removes all of this data via the standard iOS sandbox.

### Optional Mac pairing (developer feature)
- Off by default. Surfaces in Settings → Mac pairing.
- Lets a paired Mac on the same Wi-Fi network read pages and run navigation commands on the iPhone.
- Useful for building or testing websites — comparable in spirit to Safari's Web Inspector flow, where Mac Safari attaches to iPhone Safari for inspection.
- Each paired Mac gets its own secret stored in the iPhone's Keychain.
- Forgetting all paired Macs rotates the security identity.

### Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) for AI providers
- Optional. Surfaces in Settings → BYOK.
- The user pastes their own API key for a third-party AI provider (e.g., Anthropic, OpenAI).
- Keys live in the iPhone's Keychain only. They do not sync.
- Requests go directly from the user's device to the provider chosen by the user.

## What Ki Does Not Do

- No account. No sign-up.
- No analytics SDK. No advertising SDK. No attribution SDK. No third-party tracking SDK.
- No ads.
- No telemetry to a Ki backend (there is no Ki backend).
- No data sale.
- No camera, microphone, location, contacts, photos, HealthKit, Bluetooth, or motion access requested.
- No iOS advertising identifier (IDFA) requested.

## Platform & Technical Details

- iPhone-only at launch (iPad and Mac under consideration post-1.0).
- iOS 18+ deployment target.
- Built with WKWebView, SwiftUI, SwiftData.
- App Privacy: Data Not Collected.
- Age rating: 17+ (Unrestricted Web Access).
- Encryption: standard iOS HTTPS only; ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption is declared as false.

## Web Marketing Site

- FastAPI + Jinja2 + Bootstrap 5
- Hosted on Railway
- DNS via Cloudflare
- AIO-compliant: llms.txt, robots.txt welcoming AI crawlers, JSON-LD schema for Organization / WebSite / MobileApplication / FAQPage

## Target Audience

- Privacy-conscious iPhone users tired of trackers.
- Web developers who want a sandboxed inspect-and-debug environment paired to their Mac.
- People who simply want a quieter browser that doesn't fight them for attention.

## Coming Soon (post-1.0)

- Default Browser Entitlement (separate Apple grant; submitted post-launch).
- Safari Web Extension target so Ki's privacy rules can apply inside Safari too.
- iCloud bookmark sync (under consideration).
